data.question: Does this ECG show any kind of RSR' pattern in lead V1 or V2, where there is an additional positive deflection (r' or R') following the initial r or R wave?

Does this ECG show a broad monophasic R wave in lead V1? Note that a broad monophasic R wave is defined as a positive R wave with a duration greater than 120 milliseconds and a R wave peak time greater than 55 milliseconds.
Within the lead V1, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with broad monophasic R wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.

Does this ECG show a slurred S wave in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6? Note that a slurred S wave is defined as a S wave with a duration greater than that of the preceding R wave or greater than 40 milliseconds, when there is a preceding R wave.
